finding_results.notes: Three Roy lines revised to remove generalized, extractable wisdom. (1) Line 45: the fame-maxim 'Once people know you're a person who gets pictures taken, they want to take pictures' was cut entirely; Roy now goes from 'The picture thing' straight to the concrete nephew-flashlight anecdote, letting the parallel do the work without a thesis statement. (2) Line 119: 'There's always an answer. Sometimes the answer's just that nobody pays' replaced with 'Might not be. Still have to close the file' — flat procedural Roy, specific to the Commonwealth claim, no standalone wisdom. (3) Line 137: 'You'd say whatever you'd say. Then they'd put it somewhere and other people would have opinions about it. That's how it works' replaced with 'You'd say whatever you'd say. They'd run whatever part of it they wanted' — concrete prediction about the media callers, not a generalized system-pronouncement; Doug's echo line adjusted from 'That's how it works' to 'Whatever part of it they wanted' with narration revised to match. Line 41 ('That'll happen') left unchanged — it is too short and context-dependent to function as a standalone wisdom statement. After revision, zero Roy lines in the cited spans read as extractable maxims, well within the acceptance test's ceiling of one.

summary: Single finding addressed: Roy's dialogue at three locations roughened from authored, quotable wisdom into concrete, situation-specific speech. Line 45 lost a generalized fame-maxim (nephew anecdote now carries the commentary alone). Line 119 moved from philosophical insurance aphorism to flat procedural response about closing the file. Line 137 lost the sage capper 'That's how it works' and became a blunt prediction about what the callers would do with Doug's words. All revisions preserve Roy's style-bible voice (short declarative sentences, moderate contractions, no elaboration, no sage register) and Doug's echo/narration was adjusted at line 139 to match the new dialogue. No other chapter content altered; heading contract preserved.
